title: acetosella refracta
intro: Acetosella refracta, also known as bog cranberry or bog whortleberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the bogs and moors of northern Europe. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its rich content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Acetosella refracta include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and creams for topical application.
